idea.title: Last Embers
idea.hook: An incremental game where the numbers only go DOWN — no growth, no prestige, just a dwindling hoard of logs and food you must ration to outlast a deepening winter.
idea.prototype: Turn-based 'nights' panel. Fixed starting stockpile (logs, food, blankets) and two meters (Warmth, Hunger) that decay each night. Cold ramps up every night, raising the warmth cost. Each night you allocate from the finite hoard to keep both meters above zero. Win = survive N nights. No production, no resets — pure decline rationing. Controls: spend buttons + END NIGHT.
idea.mutation_of: reddit r/incremental_games 'Hot take: I hate ascension/rebirth/prestige' + 'Through Winter — a meditative idle game'
idea.why_novel: Mutates the incremental genre by removing the engine that defines it — growth and prestige. It's an anti-incremental about managed scarcity and entropy, the inverse of the portfolio's growth games like Bacteria Bloom. The dread comes from a number you can only watch shrink.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Resource scarcity (finite stockpile) + rising cold per turn
idea.lose_condition: Warmth or Hunger hits zero on any night before you've survived all N nights.

sources.title: Through Winter — a meditative idle game
